key_findings: Seven entity types defined with deterministic dedup keys: Domain, Subdomain, IPAddress, Port, HTTPEndpoint, Service, Vulnerability — each anchored by a SHA256 prefix-tagged composite key
Entity-type prefix in dedup key (e.g., 'domain:' vs 'subdomain:') is mandatory — prevents cross-type key collision when identical string values appear across entity types
CNAME aliases must be distinct nodes (not collapsed to target) — alias and target can have distinct ownership, findings, and KRIL scores; collapsing corrupts analyst attribution
SAN_PEER relationship uses MIN/MAX key ordering for structural idempotency — undirected relationship creates same key regardless of which direction is inserted first
RESOLVES_TO relationship includes batch_id in key — DNS IP assignments change over time; batch_id enables historical tracking while deduplicating within-batch duplicate inserts
Wildcard DNS detection must run at corpus load time (before queue dispatch) — filtering at parse time is too late; phantom FQDNs waste Oracle scan resources
Dynamic HTTPEndpoint body_hash: flag dynamic_content=true when body_hash changes between probes; never update body_hash on each probe for dynamic URLs
Conservative bias: phantom duplication is preferred over phantom collapse — wrong merge hides relationships irreversibly; duplicate nodes are visible and removable
Reconciliation uses ClickHouse FINAL after 30s settle delay; mismatch threshold ≤ 0.5% — extend settle to 60s if clickhouse_parts_per_table > 300
Seven-test validation suite covers dedup uniqueness, cross-entity prefix isolation, replay idempotency, CNAME isolation, wildcard suppression, link precision (≥99%), and reconciliation accuracy

decisions.title: Entity-type prefix in all dedup keys
decisions.summary: Dedup keys prefixed by entity type: 'domain:', 'subdomain:', 'ip:', 'port:', 'http:', 'service:', 'vuln:'
decisions.rationale: SHA256('example.com') would collide across Domain and Subdomain for apex domains. Prefix eliminates cross-type collision structurally.

decisions.title: CNAME aliases as distinct nodes
decisions.summary: alias.example.com CNAME target.other.com → two Subdomain nodes + CNAME_TO relationship
decisions.rationale: Alias and target can have distinct ownership, out-of-scope/in-scope classification, findings, and KRIL scores. Collapsing would corrupt analyst attribution and relationship topology.

decisions.title: RESOLVES_TO relationship includes batch_id
decisions.summary: Rel key = SHA256(subdomain_key + '→DNS→' + ip_key + batch_id)[:32] — enables historical IP assignment tracking
decisions.rationale: DNS IP assignments change. Without batch_id, a second observation of the same (fqdn, ip) pair would be deduped away, losing the temporal record. With batch_id, each batch creates a new relationship record.

decisions.title: SAN_PEER relationship uses MIN/MAX key ordering
decisions.summary: Rel key = SHA256(MIN(a_key, b_key) + '→SAN→' + MAX(a_key, b_key))[:32]
decisions.rationale: SAN co-occurrence is undirected. MIN/MAX ordering ensures the same key is produced regardless of insertion direction, preventing duplicate undirected relationships.

decisions.title: Wildcard DNS suppression at corpus load
decisions.summary: 5 synthetic probes per root domain before scan dispatch; domains with wildcard resolution flagged; FQDN matching wildcard suppressed from ingest queue
decisions.rationale: Wildcard DNS inflates corpus by resolving all synthetic FQDNs. Detection at corpus load prevents sending millions of phantom FQDNs to Oracle; filtering at parse time is too late and wastes scan resources.

decisions.title: Conservative bias: phantom duplication preferred over phantom collapse
decisions.summary: When dedup confidence is ambiguous, do not merge — create separate nodes and let analyst adjudicate
decisions.rationale: Wrong merge in Neo4j corrupts graph topology — relationships between entities are merged, hiding distinct attack surface. A phantom duplicate node is visible in reconciliation and safely removable.

decisions.title: Reconciliation per batch with 30s settle delay
decisions.summary: Run reconciliation after each AWSEM chunk completion; wait 30s for ClickHouse background merge before FINAL query
decisions.rationale: Per-record reconciliation is too expensive. Per-hour is too coarse for batch integrity. Per-batch reconciliation provides accurate mismatch detection at ingest batch granularity.

edge_cases: Multi-level eTLD (co.uk, com.au) requires PSL library — not naive split
CNAME aliases: create distinct nodes + CNAME_TO relationship; do NOT collapse
Wildcard DNS: detect 5-probe wildcard; suppress wildcard-matching FQDNs from ingest
CDN IPs: tag cdn=true if IP in CDN CIDR list; still ingest as valid entity

normalization: Strip tracking params (utm_source, utm_medium, fbclid, etc.)
edge_cases: Dynamic content: set dynamic_content=true if body_hash changes between probes; do NOT update body_hash on each probe
Same template + different evidence = TWO distinct Vulnerability nodes
Transient findings: do NOT delete if not re-confirmed; mark last_confirmed unchanged; analyst decides archival

wildcard_detection.algorithm: 5 synthetic UUID probes per root domain before dispatch
wildcard_detection.detection_condition: All 5 probes resolve to same A record set
wildcard_detection.action: Set domain.wildcard=true; filter matching FQDNs from ingest queue
wildcard_detection.execution_timing: At corpus load; re-run on unexpected_resolution_rate_spike ORS signal

reconciliation_strategy.query_pattern: ClickHouse FINAL count vs Neo4j node count for batch_id set
reconciliation_strategy.on_mismatch: ALERT ORS + Mattermost #ops-critical + halt next batch dispatch

validation_suite.name: Dedup Key Uniqueness
validation_suite.method: Generate 100k FQDNs; compute keys; assert zero SHA256 collisions

validation_suite.name: Cross-Entity Prefix Isolation
validation_suite.method: Same string across entity types; assert keys differ

validation_suite.name: Replay Idempotency
validation_suite.method: Ingest same 10k records twice; assert Neo4j + ClickHouse counts unchanged

validation_suite.name: CNAME Alias Isolation
validation_suite.method: Ingest alias + target; assert two distinct nodes + one CNAME_TO relationship

validation_suite.name: Wildcard Suppression
validation_suite.method: Wildcard domain + 1k synthetic FQDNs; assert zero nodes ingested

validation_suite.name: Link Precision
validation_suite.method: 1k known (subdomain, IP) pairs; assert >= 99% RESOLVES_TO links present; zero false links

validation_suite.name: Reconciliation Accuracy
validation_suite.method: 10k records; 30s settle; assert Neo4j within +/-0.5% of ClickHouse FINAL count

risk_model.title: Wildcard inflation before detection
risk_model.mitigation: Wildcard detection at corpus load; ORS monitors unexpected_resolution_rate_spike

risk_model.title: Dynamic body_hash causing false dedup misses
risk_model.mitigation: Flag dynamic_content on first hash change; never update body_hash for dynamic endpoints

risk_model.title: Multi-level eTLD extraction failures
risk_model.mitigation: Use PSL library; unit test against known multi-level eTLDs

risk_model.title: RESOLVES_TO relationship explosion (high-rotation IPs)
risk_model.mitigation: Implement archival flag for RESOLVES_TO relationships stale > 90 days; not deletion

risk_model.title: Reconciliation false positive from ClickHouse merge lag
risk_model.mitigation: 30s settle delay; extend to 60s if clickhouse_parts_per_table > 300

recommended_actions.action: Implement wildcard DNS detection as corpus pre-processing step before first scan dispatch
Use public suffix list (PSL) library for eTLD+1 extraction — no custom split logic
Store dedup key as key_hash property on every Neo4j node for fast lookup and audit
Run all 7 validation suite tests before first production write
Implement SAN_PEER relationship from TLS certificate SAN fields
Set reconciliation cadence to per-batch (not per-record or per-hour)
Flag dynamic_content=true on HTTPEndpoints where body_hash changes between consecutive probes
Implement CNAME_TO relationship from DNS CNAME chain records in dnsx output

assumptions: A1: eTLD+1 extraction uses Mozilla Public Suffix List; PSL library available as dependency
A2: dnsx output includes full CNAME chain for CNAME_TO relationship construction
A3: ClickHouse tables use ReplacingMergeTree(version) where version = Unix timestamp
A4: 30s settle delay sufficient for ClickHouse background merge on batches <= 100k rows
A5: Wildcard detection cost: 5 DNS queries per domain x domain count in corpus
A6: SHA256 prefix-tagged keys at 32 hex chars provide sufficient uniqueness for 10M+ entities (collision probability << 1 in 10^30)
